documents.t: Welcome to Heiman Technology! This is the official knowledge base of Heiman Technology, bringing together product documentation, technical solutions, industry news, and company updates.
The Air Quality Monitor detects multiple indoor air pollutants, helping maintain a healthy living environment.

Carbon monoxide (CO) is a colorless, odorless, non-irritating toxic gas known as the "silent killer." Heiman CO Sensor uses electrochemical sensor technology to accurately detect CO concentration in the air.
The Door/Window Sensor detects the open/close status of doors and windows, serving as a fundamental component of smart security systems.
The Emergency Button (Panic Button) is used to quickly trigger aSOS signal in emergency situations, serving as an important component of elderly care and security systems.
The Fall Detector is designed specifically for the elderly, automatically detecting fall incidents and sendingSOS signals in time, serving as a key device in smart elderly care solutions.
The Gas Detector is used to detect leaks of flammable gases such as natural gas (methane) and liquefied petroleum gas (propane), and is a core device for kitchen safety.
The Heat Detector detects abnormal temperature rises to trigger fire alarms, suitable for kitchens, garages, and other scenarios where smoke sensors are prone to false alarms.
The PIR (Passive Infrared) Sensor detects movement by sensing infrared heat radiation emitted by the human body, and is the most commonly used presence detection solution in smart homes.
The Presence Sensor (Occupancy / Presence Sensor) uses millimeter-wave radar technology to detect stationary human presence, complementing traditional PIR infrared sensors.
The Smart Siren provides high-volume audible and visual alerts, working with various sensors as an output actuator for security systems.
The Heiman Smart Doorbell integrates doorbell calling and security monitoring functions, supporting remote video intercom.
The Smart Gateway is the central hub of the Heiman smart home system, responsible for connecting various Zigbee sensors and enabling linkage control between devices.
The Heiman Smart Metering Plug supports remote on/off control and real-time power consumption monitoring, helping users achieve intelligent appliance management.
Heiman Smoke Sensor uses photoelectric smoke detection technology to sensitively detect visible smoke particles at the early stage of a fire, enabling ultra-early fire warning.
The Temperature/Humidity Sensor monitors ambient temperature and relative humidity in real time, serving as a fundamental device for smart home environmental monitoring.
The Vibration Sensor detects vibration or tilt of objects to trigger alarms, suitable for doors, windows, safes, pipes, and various other scenarios.
The Water Leak Sensor detects water leaks or accumulation, effectively preventing property damage from burst pipes or flooding.
Homey is a smart home hub developed by the Dutch company Athom, supporting Zigbee, Z-Wave, Wi-Fi, Matter, Thread and other protocols.
ZHA is the built-in Zigbee integration for Home Assistant, allowing direct management of Zigbee devices without additional software.
Zigbee2MQTT is an open-source Zigbee gateway software that bridges Zigbee devices to the MQTT protocol, enabling integration with platforms such as Home Assistant.
In February 2026, Heiman Technology officially joined the Works with Home Assistant certification program, becoming one of the first Chinese partners in the program.

Matter (formerly Project CHIP) is a smart home interoperability standard launched by the Connectivity Standards Alliance (CSA), designed to break down brand barriers and enable cross-ecosystem interoperability.

documents.s: Heiman Technology Recognized as National "Little Giant" Enterprise

documents.t: In 2022, Heiman Technology was officially recognized as a national "Little Giant" enterprise (Specialized and New) for its deep technical expertise and innovation capabilities in the smart sensing field. This national-level recognition is a high affirmation of Heiman's specialization, refinement, differentiation, and innovation. Heiman will continue to deepen its focus on smart fire safety, smart gas safety, smart elderly care, and smart sensing, consistently delivering quality products and services to customers. Previously, Heiman Technology had already received several authoritative qualifications including National High-Tech Enterprise and Guangdong Province Specialized and New SME certifications.
documents.s: Heiman Technology Recognized as National "Little Giant" Enterprise

documents.t: Since its founding in 2005, Heiman Technology has journeyed over 20 years of development. This article takes you through Heiman's key milestones.
Shenzhen Heiman Technology Co., Ltd. was officially established, entering the security and sensing field.
Multiple products received EU CE certification, and a partnership was established with China Mobile.
Multiple products passed EU EN54-7 and EN14604 certification, marking product quality reaching international advanced standards.
The smart home project was fully launched, transitioning Heiman into a company focused on R&D, production, and sales of smart home products.
Developed China's first NB-IoT smoke alarm Won 2 German Red Dot Design Awards Obtained MFi certification and Apple manufacturer qualification
Completed Series A funding round with Shenzhen HTI Group as investor, received "Guangdong Province Specialized and New SME" designation.
Recognized as a national "Little Giant" enterprise; K1 series won German Red Dot Design Award.
Heiman testing laboratory officially passed national CNAS accreditation; smoke alarms and CO alarms were among the first to receive Matter 1.2 certification.
Continuous innovation, deeply engaged in smart fire safety, smart gas safety, smart elderly care, and smart sensing, providing quality products and services to customers in 100+ countries worldwide.

Ensure sensor air intake and exhaust are not blocked Clean sensor housing periodically to prevent dust buildup Place in children's room to monitor CO₂ levels and prompt window ventilation Recommended to linkage with fresh air system or air purifier

The Gas Detector is used to detect leaks of flammable gases such as natural gas (methane) and liquefied petroleum gas (propane), and is a core device for kitchen safety.

Leak detected → shutoff valve: automatically close gas pipeline valve Alarm + exhaust: automatically turn on exhaust fan / range hood Push notification: receive leak alarm on phone in real time
If you smell gas, do NOT operate electrical switches or use open flames. Immediately open windows for ventilation and call emergency services from a safe outdoor area.

Natural gas (methane): lighter than air, install within 30 cm of ceiling Liquefied petroleum gas (propane): heavier than air, install within 30 cm of floor Near potential leak points such as gas stoves, water heaters, gas pipe joints

Confirm gas type to be detected (natural gas or LPG) Select corresponding mounting height Secure mounting base to wall with screws Connect power (most models are mains-powered) Power on sensor, allow ~3-5 minutes warm-up Self-test: press test button, verify audible and visual alarm Pair via gateway (Zigbee version) If linkage solenoid valve, connect shutoff valve control wire

Periodically test sensor sensitivity with test gas Sensor lifespan ~3-5 years, replace when expired If you smell gas, do NOT operate electrical switches or use open flames Mounting height depends on gas type (natural gas high, LPG low)

The Heat Detector detects abnormal temperature rises to trigger fire alarms, suitable for kitchens, garages, and other scenarios where smoke sensors are prone to false alarms.

The heat sensor uses thermosensitive elements to detect temperature changes: Fixed temperature alarm: triggers when temperature exceeds set threshold (typically 57°C / 135°F) Rate-of-rise alarm: triggers when temperature rise rate exceeds set value (e.g. 8.3°C/minute)
Kitchens (smoke sensors are prone to false alarms from cooking) Garages/workshops (exhaust fumes, dust may interfere with smoke sensors) Boiler rooms/heating equipment rooms Bathrooms/saunas

Abnormal temperature rise detection Tamper alarm Low battery reminder Note: Heat sensors and smoke sensors are complementary. Smoke sensors respond faster, while heat sensors have better anti-interference capability.

Ceiling mount, center of room Best use scenarios: kitchens, garages, boiler rooms, bathrooms — where smoke sensors are prone to false alarms At least 30 cm from walls Avoid installing near vents, AC outlets

Select mounting location (recommended: kitchen or garage ceiling) Secure mounting base to ceiling Insert battery, sensor powers on automatically Rotate and snap main unit into base Self-test: press test button, LED lights up and sounds Pair via gateway (Zigbee version)

Heat sensor is not a replacement for smoke sensors, but a complement Smoke sensors respond faster, heat sensors have better interference immunity Recommend installing both smoke and heat sensors in the same room Test once a month, replace battery promptly when low

The Presence Sensor (Occupancy / Presence Sensor) uses millimeter-wave radar technology to detect stationary human presence, complementing traditional PIR infrared sensors.
Series Communication Features HS8OS Zigbee Millimeter-wave radar presence detection

Unlike PIR sensors that can only detect movement, presence sensors transmit and receive millimeter-wave signals, using the micro-Doppler effect to detect tiny body movements (such as chest movements from breathing), thereby determining if someone is present.

Stationary presence detection: detects lying down, sitting still, and other micro-movements Detection range: covers radius of 3-5 meters Ceiling mount: optimal detection angle Complements PIR: PIR handles wide-area motion detection, presence sensor handles fine-grained detection

Smart lighting: lights stay on when someone is present, auto-off when vacant (solves PIR lights-off issue when stationary) HVAC control: auto on/off based on room occupancy Elderly care: prolonged stillness in bathroom/bedroom triggers system alert Office energy saving: auto-adjust energy when meeting rooms/workstations are unoccupied

Homey is a smart home hub developed by the Dutch company Athom, supporting Zigbee, Z-Wave, Wi-Fi, Matter, Thread and other protocols.

Heiman Zigbee and Z-Wave devices can be paired directly via Homey's built-in wireless chip, no additional gateway required. Matter devices can be connected through Homey Pro's built-in Thread border router.

Multi-protocol all-in-one: Homey Pro supports Zigbee, Z-Wave, Thread, and Wi-Fi simultaneously Local operation: All automations run locally Flow Automation: Drag-and-drop programming to create complex automation scenarios Reference: Search for Heiman-related apps in the Homey App Store
ZHA is the built-in Zigbee integration for Home Assistant, allowing direct management of Zigbee devices without additional software.

Heiman Zigbee sensors have excellent compatibility with ZHA, supporting: Smoke/CO Alarm: Alarm status, fault detection, battery level, silence Door/Window Sensor: Open/close, tamper PIR Sensor: Motion detection, illuminance Temperature & Humidity: Temperature, humidity Water Leak: Water leak alarm Smart Plug: On/off, power metering
Zigbee Coordinator: HUSBZB-1, Conbee II, SkyConnect, SLZB-06, etc. Connected to Home Assistant via USB or network

ZHA configuration is simple — select the serial port and region, and it will automatically scan and discover devices. No manual configuration file editing required. Reference: Home Assistant ZHA Documentation
Zigbee2MQTT is an open-source Zigbee gateway software that bridges Zigbee devices to the MQTT protocol, enabling integration with platforms such as Home Assistant.

Heiman Zigbee sensors have excellent compatibility with Zigbee2MQTT — most devices are automatically recognized and support all key functions: Smoke/Carbon Monoxide Alarm: Alarm status, fault detection, silence control, battery level Sensors: Temperature, humidity, illuminance, occupancy detection Door/Window Sensor: Open/close status, tamper detection
CC2652P / CC1352P series coordinators (e.g. SLZB-06, TubeZB, ZigStar) Heiman HS6GW series Zigbee gateways can also be used

Shenzhen Heiman Technology Co., Ltd. (Heiman) was founded in 2005 and is a high-tech enterprise focused on the smart home security field. Headquartered in Shenzhen, China, with over 20 years of industry experience, its products are exported to Europe, Americas, Asia, and other regions worldwide.

Heiman Technology's mission is "Safeguard with craftsmanship, warm life with technology." Its main business areas include: Smart Fire Safety: Smoke alarms, carbon monoxide alarms, gas detectors, heat detectors, etc. Smart Gas Safety: Gas leak detection and remote shut-off solutions Smart Elderly Care: Fall detection, emergency call, and elderly safety monitoring solutions Smart Sensing: PIR sensors, presence sensors, door/window sensors, temperature/humidity sensors, water leak sensors, air quality sensors, etc.

Heiman is an early adopter of the Matter protocol, launching the world's first Matter-certified carbon monoxide alarm. Heiman's Matter devices all use the Matter over Thread solution.

Wi-Fi devices are suitable for: Environments with established network infrastructure, no need for an extra gateway Devices with low power sensitivity (e.g., smart plugs) Scenarios requiring remote access and cloud services Homes with mature Wi-Fi Mesh network coverage

Wi-Fi sensor battery consumption is higher than Zigbee/Thread A large number of Wi-Fi devices may affect network stability HEIMAN's main product line focuses on Zigbee / Matter over Thread; the Wi-Fi product line is relatively streamlined

wM-Bus (Wireless Meter-Bus) is a European standard (EN 13757) wireless meter reading protocol widely used for remote data collection of energy metering devices such as water meters, gas meters, electricity meters, and heat meters.

Feature Description Standard EN 13757 Operating Frequency 868 MHz (EU standard) Transmission Range 100-500 m (open area) Power Consumption Very low, battery life up to 10 years Primary Application Energy metering, centralized meter reading

Centralized apartment/residential gas meter reading Commercial building energy consumption monitoring Smart city infrastructure Note: wM-Bus is a vertical industry protocol primarily used in Heiman's gas and fire protection project solutions, and is not applicable to ordinary home smart home scenarios.

Zigbee is a low-power, low-data-rate wireless Mesh network protocol based on the IEEE 802.15.4 standard. It is one of the most mature and widely adopted protocols in the smart home domain.

Heiman is among the first Chinese manufacturers to adopt the Zigbee protocol, with an extensive Zigbee product line. Heiman Zigbee devices are compatible with the Zigbee 3.0 standard and can interoperate with mainstream Zigbee gateways and coordinators.

Feature Description Frequency Band 2.4 GHz (Global) Data Rate Up to 250 kbps Network Topology Mesh Self-Healing Network Node Capacity Up to 65,535 nodes per network Communication Range Approx. 10-30 m indoors (depending on obstacles) Power Consumption Ultra-low; sensor battery life up to 3 years

Heiman Zigbee devices can be integrated with the following platforms: Zigbee2MQTT — Open-source Zigbee to MQTT bridge ZHA — Home Assistant built-in Zigbee integration Hubitat — Local smart home hub Homey — Multi-protocol smart home hub SmartThings Hub — Samsung smart home hub Tuya Zigbee Gateway — Tuya ecosystem Heiman HS6GW Gateway — Official gateway

The CE (Conformité Européenne) mark is a mandatory safety mark for products entering the EU market.
The CE mark indicates that a product meets the essential requirements of relevant EU directives and can circulate freely within EU member states and the European Economic Area (EEA).
Heiman smoke/CO alarms meet CE requirements through the following routes:
documents.s: CE Certification Path for Heiman Products

documents.t: The Construction Products Regulation requires fire alarm products to undergo performance stability assessment: Applicable standards: EN 14604 (smoke), EN 50291 (CO) Assessment body: TÜV Rheinland and other notified bodies Certification level: DoP (Declaration of Performance) + CE mark

The Radio Equipment Directive requires wireless devices: Covers wireless modules such as Zigbee, Z-Wave, Wi-Fi, Thread Test items: Electromagnetic Compatibility (EMC), Radio Frequency (RF), SAR, Safety
The Electromagnetic Compatibility Directive ensures that equipment does not cause interference to other electronic devices.

All Heiman products sold to the EU market bear the CE mark, including: HS1SA-E / HS3SA (Zigbee smoke alarm) HS1CA-E / C1 series (Zigbee CO alarm) HS1SA-M / HS1CA-M (Matter over Thread) HS1MS / HS1DS and the full range of Zigbee sensors

EN (European Norm) is a technical standard developed by the European Committee for Standardization (CEN) and the European Committee for Electrotechnical Standardization (CENELEC). The main EN standards relevant to Heiman products are as follows:

Item Description Scope of Application Standalone smoke alarm Test Requirements Photoelectric smoke detection, audible and visual alarm, low battery indication Applicable Markets EU member states, UK, Switzerland, Norway, etc. Heiman Products HS1SA-E, HS3SA, HS1SA-M, S1 series, etc. Certification Significance Meets EU building fire safety regulations, a prerequisite for European market access Test items: Response time, sound pressure level (≥85 dB/3m), silence function, battery life, temperature cycling, humidity endurance, RF interference immunity, etc.

Item Description Scope of Application Household carbon monoxide (CO) alarm Test Requirements Electrochemical CO sensor, concentration-graded alarm Applicable Markets EU member states, UK Heiman Products HS1CA-E, C1 series, HS1CA-M, etc. Certification Significance Ensures reliability of CO leak detection, mandatory installation in some countries

EN 62368-1: Audio/video and IT equipment safety EN 301 489: Electromagnetic Compatibility (EMC) EN 300 220: Short Range Device (SRD) radio spectrum EN 62479: Assessment of human exposure to low-frequency electromagnetic fields
GB (Guóbiāo, national standard) is the abbreviation for national standards of the People's Republic of China, formulated by the standardization administrative department of the State Council.

Standard No. Standard Name Applicable Products GB 20517 Independent Smoke Fire Detector Alarm Smoke alarm GB 15322.1 Combustible Gas Detector Part 1: Industrial and Commercial Use Industrial gas detector GB 15322.2 Combustible Gas Detector Part 2: Household Use Household gas detector GB/T 31141 Household Carbon Monoxide Alarm CO alarm GB 4706.1 Safety of Household and Similar Electrical Appliances Smart plug
documents.s: Main GB Standards for Heiman Products

documents.t: CCC certification (China Compulsory Certification) is technically based on GB standards. A product that passes CCC certification is verified to comply with the relevant GB standard requirements.

Q-Label is a quality certification mark for the European smart home industry, launched by the Q-Lab Alliance (now part of the Zigbee Alliance / CSA Connectivity Standards Alliance). Item Description Scope of Application Zigbee device interoperability certification Certification Content Compatibility of devices with other brands' Zigbee gateways/controllers Significance Ensures cross-brand interoperability, reducing the risk of "not working after purchase" Heiman Zigbee products are Q-Label certified, ensuring compatibility with the following platforms: Amazon Echo Plus / Echo Show (built-in Zigbee) Philips Hue Bridge SmartThings Hub Other Q-Label certified gateways
